text.title: E2E Framework Migration Playbook Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying e2e-framework-migration-playbook. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Wave 2 runs for 9 months; nobody starts Wave 3 because the team is shipping features instead.
antipattern.root-cause: No exit criteria + no time-box on Wave 2; dual-run feels safe enough to live in forever.
antipattern.fix: Set a maximum Wave 2 duration (e.g. 12 weeks). Force a go/no-go gate at the duration cap. Either flip to cutover or abort the migration.

antipattern.symptom: Codemod applied to 200 files; 17 broke in subtle ways nobody noticed until a customer report.
antipattern.root-cause: Codemod authored against happy path; edge cases not enumerated.
antipattern.fix: Author codemod test fixtures covering every observed AST shape before running on production code. Run codemod on the test set first; reject if any case regresses.

antipattern.symptom: Migration declared 'done' at Wave 3; old framework sits in repo for years, blocking future migrations.
antipattern.root-cause: Wave 4 felt optional; engineering moved to next feature instead of cleanup.
antipattern.fix: Make Wave 4 a tracked deliverable on the migration ADR. Block the migration from 'closed' status until decommission lands.

antipattern.symptom: Wave 3 cutover went wrong; rollback procedure had never been exercised; recovery took 4 hours.
antipattern.fix: Run a rollback drill on staging before every cutover wave. Time it; require <=30 min from incident detection to restored old path.
